---
from: markdown+lists_without_preceding_blankline
title: "Agentic Architects"
modified: 2026-05-15
date: 2026-05-04
public: true
tags:
  - Concepts
  - Study
  - Ai
---

>[!summary] Todo List
> 
> - [x] #todo #reading #tier1 Read [Agentic Architects](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.25083). (Carnegie Mellon Apr 2026) 🆔 t00tzp ✅ 2026-05-06
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1 Read [ArchAgent](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22425) (Google Feb 2026) 🆔 ek9qli ⛔ t00tzp
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1 Read [ChatArch](https://dl.acm.org/doi/epdf/10.1145/3774888) (Fudan U, China, Dec 2025) 🆔 9bojyc ⛔ ek9qli
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1  Read [Computer Architecture's AlphaZero Moment](Computer Architecture's AlphaZero Moment.qmd)
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1 Read LLM-guided bottleneck analysis (LUMINA) ⛔ 9bojyc
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1 Read AdaEvolve (evolutionary LLM swarms framework)
> - [ ] #todo #reading #tier1 Read ArchExplorer


Karpathy introduced LLM swarms that optimize NN architectures. 
This was then explored by [this blog post for cacheless pipelined RISCV beating VexRISCV](https://github.com/FeSens/auto-arch-tournament).

Carnegie Mellon followed up immediately with [Agentic Architects](https://arxiv.org/html/2604.25083).

> The accepted wins are qualitatively interesting: not hyperparameter changes but structural interventions like the cold multi-cycle DIV/REM isolation (which the agent didn't know would halve LUT count; it found out by doing it) and the registered lookahead I-fetch replay predictor — a non-obvious combination of replay mechanics and fetch-side lookahead that sits outside what a typical architect would enumerate as a hypothesis class.
> --- Claude on `auto-arch-tournament`

Typical DSE is limited by the "hypothesis class".
There is a set of possible hardware, and we create DSE to find the optimal hardware within those possibilities.

On 2026-03-31, [Nvidia published a position paper](Computer%20Architecture's%20AlphaZero%20Moment) on the onset of LLMs in the exploration of the architecture design space. 

## Potentials

>[!check] Gap: ZigZag with Agentic Exploration?
> It may be a good idea to check how agent exploration can interact with the ZigZag cost models. ZigZag can serve as the "verifier", the agent can explore within the available YAMLs.
> - [ ] #todo #idea Agentic architects with ZigZag as verifier


>[!check] Gap: MiniQwen Agentic?
> Locking the features to high-cost models is bad. If the only risk for smaller models is that they typically don't come up with verification-passing architectures, the testing must be tiered and fast. 
> - [ ] #todo #idea Many little Agentic Architects
